_meta.what_firm_does: Replit is a browser-based software-creation platform (and AI agent) whose mission is to make building software accessible to everyone, including non-programmers. Foster City, CA based; operates at the intersection of AI and developer tooling.
_meta.jd_wants: Someone to build an early-career / campus recruiting program from scratch — the playbooks, events, metrics, and feedback loops — owning the v1 and improving it each season against an academic-calendar timeline.
_meta.status: DRAFT — Jamie to review/edit before submit

Why do you want to work at Replit?: Building an early-career recruiting program from the ground up isn't just about running events on a calendar—from my experience, it's about designing systems that compound: playbooks, metrics, and feedback loops that get better every cycle. What draws me to Replit is the mission to make software creation accessible to everyone, paired with the chance to build the emerging-talent engine from scratch at the intersection of AI and recruiting. At InGenius Prep I stand up and own the end-to-end roadmap for 20+ programs and build the documented playbooks and SOPs that let teams scale without me in the room — which cut onboarding time by 75%. That ship-the-v1, improve-it-each-season instinct is exactly what this role calls for.
Tell us about your experience with campu: At Kronos Research I worked the recruiting funnel firsthand — building talent pipelines, owning campus recruiting events end-to-end, and moderating C-suite panels at top universities that reached 230+ students. Through personalized outreach I grew applications by 566% in two months. The lesson that stuck: new-grad recruiting runs on an academic calendar, and the fall season is unforgiving — so you plan ahead and protect the timeline rather than improvising in the moment. That's the operating reality of building an emerging-talent program, and I've lived its rhythm, not just read about it.
How would you measure the success of an: I'd run it the way I ran data work at NextGen Healthcare, where I analyzed 2,000+ employee experiences and tracked the metrics that translated field signal into C-suite decisions. For an early-talent program that means offer acceptance, time-to-hire, funnel conversion by source, and retention of the cohorts you bring in — plus qualitative signal from candidates and hiring managers on whether the experience actually felt good. The point of retrospectives isn't a dashboard; it's deciding what to keep and what to cut next season. Diagnose with data, adjust the playbook, measure again — that's how I'd keep program health honest.
What excites you about building somethin: The part I genuinely enjoy is turning informal knowledge into repeatable systems. At InGenius I run regular listening sessions with 25+ stakeholders to surface gaps and design what's next, then build the SOPs that make it stick. Building from scratch means I get to do that without inheriting someone else's assumptions. My approach is grounded in my MS in Applied Organizational Psychology (USC): I diagnose with data, collaborate cross-functionally on the solution, and track metrics to make sure progress is real. As an analytical organizer with a growth mindset, building Replit's early-career engine is the kind of ownership I want — I'd love to build it with you.
